assumptions: No Vault server or client configuration exists yet.
The developer has not chosen Docker versus a local Vault binary, a client language, or HCP versus self-managed versus local dev mode.
There is no SaaS account creation step on the local developer quick start; account-like boundaries instead mean obtaining a runnable Vault and credentials.

developer_goal: Start Vault and use a client library or CLI to store and retrieve a first secret value.

first_success.outcome: A first secret is written to and retrieved from Vault with the documented success confirmation.
first_success.observable_signal: For the developer quick start sample languages, the program prints Access granted! after the retrieved password equals Hashi123.

route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete local path only after install method and client language are fixed; do not invent a winner among peer language tabs or HCP versus local ops routes.

candidates.name: Developer quick start with Docker and Go client
candidates.reason: Go is the first documented client library option, but Docker versus binary and other languages remain peer choices.

candidates.name: Developer quick start with another supported language
candidates.reason: Ruby, C#, Python, Java, Bash, and OpenAPI variants are presented as peer tabs with different install and code steps.

candidates.name: Operations quick start or HCP Vault Dedicated
candidates.reason: Ops CLI and hosted HCP paths reach related secret outcomes through different surfaces and prerequisites.

required_path.action: Obtain a runnable Vault by installing the binary or preparing Docker for the hashicorp/vault image.

required_fields.label: Install method
required_fields.notes: Exact package-manager or Docker Desktop prompts are OS-specific and unverified.

required_path.observable_result: The vault binary or Docker engine can run the documented start command.

required_path.action: Start Vault in non-production dev mode with the documented root token.
Install the selected language client library and write the authenticate, store, and retrieve sample.

required_fields.label: Client language and library install command
Vault address and token in client code

required_path.observable_result: Running the sample writes the secret and prints Access granted! after retrieval.

prerequisites.description: Docker or a local Vault binary is required before the developer quick start can start a server.
A development environment for one of the listed languages is required for the client-library path.

unavoidable_waits.description: First-time Docker image pull for hashicorp/vault may wait on network download before the server starts.

platform_outcomes.description: Dev-mode Vault starts unsealed in memory and accepts the configured root token.
KV v2 accepts the written secret and returns it on read for the sample path.

excluded.item: Production hardening and non-root auth methods
excluded.reason: The quick start explicitly labels dev mode insecure and points to other examples for production-like auth.

excluded.item: Reading hello-vault demo repositories as a required path
excluded.reason: Those demos are presented as additional examples after first success.

sources.title: Install Vault
evidence_supported: OS package-manager install options for the Vault binary

uncertainties.question: Which install method and client language define the measured Vault first mile?
uncertainties.impact: Docker versus binary and Go versus other language tabs change prerequisite actions and implementation fields.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Pick one bounded path such as Docker plus Go, then verify install through Access granted! on a clean workstation.

uncertainties.question: How should the corpus treat Vault's lack of a SaaS account-creation form relative to the account creation boundary?
uncertainties.impact: Whether install counts as the account-equivalent first action affects comparability with hosted identity products.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A selection-policy decision for self-hosted CLIs with no vendor signup, plus a fresh-machine count of required install prompts.
